summary: Joel announces a simple programming challenge for the Stack Overflow community: create a full-screen countdown timer application for use during StackOverflow DevDays conference intermissions. The app needs to display on the main screen to warn attendees when sessions are about to resume. He frames it as both a practical need for keeping the packed conference schedule on time and as a way to showcase the community's programming talent. The contest is hosted on Meta Stack Overflow.
key_points: StackOverflow DevDays was a series of one-day mini-conferences held in ten cities, requiring tight schedule management
Joel turned a practical conference need into a community engagement opportunity by posting it as a contest
The challenge demonstrates Joel's philosophy of leveraging community talent for real-world problems
money_quotes: I thought this would be a great opportunity to showcase the StackOverflow community's programming talent, so I posted a little contest over on meta.
key_insight: Even mundane operational needs like a countdown timer can be turned into community engagement opportunities that showcase developer talent.
